actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour (script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; class Fire) on this GameObject matches the provided settings (isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2). This GameObject has a BoxCollider (no Rigidbody). Goal: cover the auto-ignition path in Update via neighbor pressure and CheckNeighborState. Pre-steps required: (1) Ensure neighbors list is populated (external system must call FindNeighbors(Fire[] allFires, int fireId)). (2) Ensure at least two neighbors within 4 units report as on by calling NotifyNeighborState(true) twice (or having neighbors toggle to on and notify), ideally with one neighbor lower by >3 units (y difference) to set hasDownNeighbor=true. Expectation during the wait: shouldTurnOn=true; timeToOnDelta accumulates until reaching timeToOn (random in [40,60]; worst-case 60s, halved if hasDownNeighbor). On completion: isOn becomes true; UpdateFireState(true) runs, which plays particle systems, notifies neighbors (NotifyAllNeighbors) and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1).

Precondition: Fire is already on from prior step (isOn=true). Purpose: cover partial extinguish flow start. While this trigger is active, an external water system must continuously call Fire.WaterHit(Vector3 normal) with intervals < maxHitInterval (2s). Expectation: isTurningOff=true, currentHitInterval resets to 0 on each hit, currentHitTime accumulates but stays < totalHitTime (5s). No turn-off yet.

Immediately after the previous water spray stops, wait > maxHitInterval (2.0s) with no further WaterHit calls. Expectation from Update: currentHitInterval exceeds maxHitInterval, causing isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime reset to 0 without turning the fire off. This covers the interval-timeout branch.

Precondition: Ensure fire is on (if needed, some external controller must call SetIsOn(true) once to light it). During this trigger window, an external water system must repeatedly call WaterHit(Vector3 normal) so that currentHitInterval never reaches maxHitInterval until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). Expectation: In Update, once currentHitTime >= 5, the fire turns off: isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0; UpdateFireState(true) runs, which stops particle systems, notifies neighbors (NotifyAllNeighbors) and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). This covers the full extinguish branch.

Optional direct state toggle to cover UpdateFireState(false) no-notify path. External test harness should manually invoke SetIsOn(false) then SetIsOn(true) on this component (both methods require a bool parameter). Expectation: Particle systems stop/play accordingly, but NotifyAllNeighbors and FireController.FireChanged are NOT invoked because UpdateFireState(false) is used.
